factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (US-Iran strikes)
factors.summary: US launched 'powerful strikes' on Iran after tanker attacks in Strait of Hormuz; Treasury revoked Iran oil sales waiver (granted June 21, revoked July 7). Risk of supply shock and elevated oil volatility; Exxon Q2 earnings up ~$3.7B from war-driven rally, but systemic uncertainty is negative for equities.

factors.name: Chip sector weakness and AI reversal
factors.summary: Samsung earnings disappointed investors; Nasdaq fell -1.16% (-302 pts), semiconductor ETF (SMH) down ~5%. Signals potential cooling in AI hardware demand cycle. Heavy Dow exposure via MSFT, and broader tech rotation concerns.

factors.name: Oil price spike
factors.summary: WTI crude rising on Hormuz supply risk and Iran sanctions. Near-term bullish for energy stocks (CVX in Dow), but stagflationary if sustained; input costs pressurize industrial and consumer segments.

factors.name: Fed policy tone (soft inflation narrative)
factors.summary: Fed's Williams sounded more upbeat on inflation as oil prices retreated earlier in week. One-year inflation expectations hit highest since 2023 in NY Fed survey, but Williams comments suggest room for dovish pivot. Reduces near-term rate-hike risk.

factors.name: Corporate bond issuance stress
factors.summary: Amazon plans $25B bond sale; AI-related debt sold off sharply. Rising borrowing costs and potential liquidity stress in credit markets. Negative for equities and rate-sensitive Dow segments (financials, utilities).

factors.name: Dollar strength / safe-haven flows
factors.summary: USD/major pairs firmed on geopolitical uncertainty (AUD down 0.39%, GBP nine-session rally ended). Dollar strength headwind for Dow's ~50% international revenue, but domestic earnings benefit partially offset. Mixed signal.

factors.name: Price action: minimal 24h move
factors.summary: Dow down only -0.17% over 24h despite major headline volatility. S&P 500 closed down -0.45%, Nasdaq down -1.16%. Indicates sector rotation (tech down, energy up) but index-level momentum muted; market digesting conflicting signals.

factors.name: Trade/tariff uncertainty
factors.summary: Latin American countries and steelmakers arguing for US tariff exemptions; Trump administration tariff rhetoric ongoing. Dow has 50% intl revenue exposure; tariff escalation could weigh on industrial/materials earnings.

factors.name: Earnings and valuation anchor
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings released in this window; some form-4s and insider trades noted but no earnings surprises. Sentiment data scarce; market awaiting next earnings cycle and macro data (ISM, factory orders, payroll proxies).

factors.name: Upcoming RBNZ rate decision (July 8)
factors.summary: Reserve Bank of New Zealand expected to hike OCR to 2.50%; minor ripple for global rate expectations but not directly Dow-material. Could signal hawkish tone elsewhere, offsetting Fed dovishness.

screening.narrative: The Dow faces conflicting headwinds: geopolitical escalation in Iran (US strikes, Hormuz supply risk, oil spike) and chip/AI sector weakness (Samsung miss, Nasdaq selloff) are offsetting a slightly softer Fed inflation narrative and safe-haven dollar inflows. Energy stocks (CVX) are supported by oil, but industrial/tech/financial heavyweights (MSFT, CAT, JPM, UNH) face headwinds from higher input costs, credit stress, tariff risk, and dampened AI enthusiasm. The index has barely moved in 24h (-0.17%), suggesting investors are in wait-and-see mode ahead of ISM/durable goods data and next earnings cycle. Phase 2: near-term chop as market digests conflicting signals.
catalysts_ahead: ISM Manufacturing PMI (next week expected) — key read on industrial health
Durable goods orders and factory orders — input to capex and manufacturing demand
Dow component earnings (UNH, GS, JPM, V, etc.) — beats/misses and guidance on tariffs, margins
Fed speakers (Williams, others) — hawkish/dovish tone on rates
Treasury yield moves (10Y, 2Y) — rate expectations and duration sensitivity
Oil price action — Hormuz risk premium, Iran sanctions enforcement
China PMI / economic data — demand for Dow cyclicals (CAT, DE, BA)
Trade policy updates — tariff announcements or negotiations
screening.risk_warning: If Hormuz supply shock worsens (full strait closure, oil >$120/bbl), stagflation fears could spike, forcing equities lower despite dovish Fed tone. Conversely, if Iran tensions de-escalate and chip sector stabilizes (NVDA earnings beat, new AI cycle data), Dow could rally sharply. Major earnings misses from heavyweights (MSFT, JPM, UNH) or hawkish Fed surprise would overturn the neutral bias to bearish. Watch credit spreads closely; widening spreads = liquidity stress risk.
screening.entry_guidance: Dow is in consolidation; wait for either (1) ISM/durable goods beat + Fed dovish repricing (enter long on retest of 52,850 support), or (2) clear chip sector stabilization (NVDA earnings, AI guidance). If oil breaches $110/bbl on Hormuz, consider defensive shorts. Move is early (Phase 2), so avoid aggressive chase; better to scale in on retracements.
